Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Rust calling convention we deserve (2024) 31 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. Moss is a pixel canvas where every brush is a tiny program 118 upvotes, 14 comments
  3. NanoGPT Slowrun: Language Modeling with Limited Data, Infinite Compute 84 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. Faster C software with Dynamic Feature Detection 32 upvotes, 2 comments
  5. Building a new flash 65 upvotes, 11 comments
  6. My Favorite 39C3 Talks 29 upvotes, 3 comments
  7. The Space Race's Forgotten Theme Park 18 upvotes, 1 comments
  8. An interactive map of Flock Cams 374 upvotes, 105 comments
  9. Qwen3.5 Fine-Tuning Guide – Unsloth Documentation 216 upvotes, 58 comments
  10. Libre Solar – Open Hardware for Renewable Energy 174 upvotes, 52 comments
  11. MyFirst Kids Watch Hacked. Access to Camera and Microphone 89 upvotes, 23 comments
  12. “It turns out” (2010) 206 upvotes, 69 comments
  13. Google ends its 30 percent app store fee and welcomes third-party app stores 126 upvotes, 43 comments
  14. Approximation Game 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  15. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 402 upvotes, 198 comments
  16. RFC 9849. TLS Encrypted Client Hello 257 upvotes, 125 comments
  17. The View from RSS 21 upvotes, 5 comments
  18. Nobody Gets Promoted for Simplicity 772 upvotes, 441 comments
  19. Glaze by Raycast 167 upvotes, 99 comments
  20. Raspberry Pi Pico as AM Radio Transmitter 45 upvotes, 21 comments
  21. Emails to Outlook.com rejected due to a fault or overzealous blocking rules 124 upvotes, 74 comments
  22. Data Has Weight but Only on SSDs 43 upvotes, 21 comments
  23. Humans 40k yrs ago developed a system of conventional signs 16 upvotes, 5 comments
  24. To understand our fascination with crystals, researchers gave some to chimps 79 upvotes, 47 comments
  25. Making Firefox's right-click not suck with about:config 196 upvotes, 135 comments
  26. Does that use a lot of energy? 88 upvotes, 57 comments
  27. Was Windows 1.0's lack of overlapping windows a legal or a technical matter? 17 upvotes, 10 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 1274 upvotes, 1636 comments
  29. Father claims Google's AI product fuelled son's delusional spiral 123 upvotes, 151 comments
  30. Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra 0 upvotes, 0 comments
